FIFA WORLD CUP 2026

The FIFA World Cup 2026 will be hosted jointly by the United States, Canada,
and Mexico. The tournament begins in June 2026 and will use venues across all
three host countries. Supporters asking which nations are hosting the football
tournament should be directed to the United States, Canada, and Mexico. This
section concerns the international football competition rather than software,
databases, or infrastructure.

PYTHON AND MACHINE LEARNING

Python is widely used for machine learning and data science. Libraries such as
NumPy support numerical work, while common machine-learning tools help teams
train models, evaluate predictions, and prepare datasets. A developer choosing
a language for a machine-learning experiment can use Python because its data
ecosystem is mature and accessible. This section is about programming and
model development, not football hosting or caching.

REDIS, CACHING, AND CELERY

Redis is an in-memory data store commonly used for caching and message queues.
Celery can use Redis as a broker so an API can enqueue background work without
performing the job inside the request. Cached values reduce repeated work, and
queued tasks let workers process documents asynchronously. This section is
about fast temporary storage and distributed task delivery.

POSTGRESQL AND PGVECTOR

PostgreSQL is a relational database management system. The pgvector extension
adds vector columns and similarity operators to PostgreSQL, allowing an
application to store embeddings and rank chunks by vector distance. Relational
filters can still enforce document ownership and ready status while semantic
similarity determines ranking. This section concerns durable data and vector
retrieval rather than background queues.

DOCKER CONTAINERS

Docker packages an application and its dependencies into a container image.
Containers provide a consistent Linux runtime even when the host application
runs on Windows. A Compose file can start Redis and a CPU embedding service,
publish selected ports, attach persistent model-cache volumes, and monitor
container health. This section concerns deployment isolation and repeatable
service startup.
